name: Mind Map App — State Review
description: Global review of the app: current features, technical health, UX gaps, and next directions.

nodes.title: Mind Map App — State Review
nodes.comment: Comprehensive review of the current state of the application, its technical health, identified gaps, and prioritized next directions.

children.title: Current Features
children.comment: All features currently implemented and working in production.

children.title: Canvas & Navigation
children.comment: React Flow canvas with zoom, pan, and fitView. Arrow key panning (150px per press, animated). Map lock toggle — nodes cannot be dragged when locked (default: locked). Click-opens-panel toggle for opening the detail panel on node click.

children.title: React Flow canvas — zoom, pan, fitView
Node System
children.comment: Hierarchical tree with unlimited depth. Nodes carry priority (left border) and status (right border) as colored edge indicators — bookending the card visually. Add, delete, expand, and collapse supported.

children.title: Layout Engine
children.comment: Dagre auto-layout computes positions from the tree structure. Supports LR (left-right), RL (right-left), and TB (top-bottom) directions. Direction and display mode are persisted per project in SQLite.

children.title: Detail Panel
children.comment: Right-side panel for full node inspection. Keyboard-driven: Enter to open, Escape to close. Title, Comment, and Status & Priority sections open by default. Comment auto-saves on blur with a brief '✓ Saved' indicator.

children.title: Title + Comment + Status open by default
Dates, days spent, code block, AI prompt, CLI command
Theme System
children.comment: Two themes defined in themes.ts with full color token tables. IBM Carbon dark uses dot background. Dusk (navy/blue-gray, inspired by ComfyUI) uses a fine grid background. ReactFlow controls themed via CSS class scoping (.theme-dusk).

children.title: CSS class scoping for ReactFlow controls
Animated Edges
children.comment: ReactFlow animated dashes flowing from parent to child. Depth-based colors: blue for depth-1, cyan for depth-2, indigo for depth-3+. Semi-transparent rgba values keep the effect visible but not distracting on both themes.

children.title: Flowing dash animation on all edges
Progress Tracking
children.comment: Status cycling (pending → in-progress → completed) cascades to all descendants. Progress shown in three places: header progress bar, floating badge (bottom-right), and per-project bars in the sidebar.

children.title: Data & Persistence
children.comment: SQLite backend via better-sqlite3 (synchronous API). Layout direction and display mode saved per project. Idempotent migrations run on every server startup so existing databases are upgraded without data loss.

children.title: Layout + display mode saved per project
Idempotent DB migrations on server startup
Technical Health
children.comment: Assessment of code quality, architecture soundness, and areas of technical debt.

children.title: Strengths
children.comment: The codebase is clean, well-typed, and follows consistent patterns throughout.

children.title: Clean TypeScript throughout — no any leaks
Single Zustand store — no prop drilling
Stale-closure ref pattern for event handlers
children.comment: clickOpensPanelRef, detailPanelOpenRef updated each render so keydown handlers never go stale without re-registration.

children.title: Tech Debt & Risks
children.comment: Known issues that need attention before scaling the codebase further.

children.title: NODE_DIMS height must be calibrated manually
children.comment: If any content is added to MindMapNode.tsx that increases rendered height, Dagre will overlap nodes until NODE_DIMS.height is updated to match.

children.title: Theme not persisted across sessions
children.comment: Theme resets to IBM on every page refresh. Should be saved in localStorage.

children.title: Edge colors hardcoded — not theme-aware
children.comment: dagreLayout.ts uses fixed rgba colors. Should read from the active theme.

children.title: DetailPanel colors not themed for Dusk
children.comment: DetailPanel still uses IBM dark colors (#161616, #1e1e1e). Contrast is acceptable but not ideal in Dusk mode.

children.title: No undo / redo system
children.comment: Accidental deletes cannot be recovered. Zustand temporal middleware (zustand/middleware) could add undo/redo without structural changes.

children.title: UX Gaps
children.comment: Features and interactions that are missing or incomplete from the user's perspective.

children.title: No inline title editing on canvas
children.comment: Must open the detail panel to rename a node. Double-click to edit title inline would be much faster for quick renames.

children.title: No drag-and-drop reparenting
children.comment: Nodes cannot be moved to a different parent. Requires React Flow's onNodeDrag + a drop-target detection system.

children.title: No search / filter nodes
children.comment: On large maps it is impossible to find a specific node quickly. A search bar that highlights or zooms to matching nodes is needed.

children.title: No node enter/exit animation
children.comment: Expanding a node's children makes them appear instantly. A fade-in or scale-up would feel more polished. Requires Framer Motion AnimatePresence or CSS @keyframes on mount.

children.title: No keyboard shortcut for new child node
children.comment: Currently requires hovering the node and clicking the + button. A keyboard shortcut (e.g. N) when a node is selected would be faster.

children.title: Detail panel not scrollable on small screens
children.comment: On MacBook or small viewports the panel content may overflow without visible scrollbar.

children.title: Next Directions
children.comment: Prioritized list of improvements. High priority items address daily friction. Medium priority items improve completeness. Future items are exploratory.

children.title: High Priority
children.comment: These items cause daily friction and should be addressed first.

children.title: Inline title editing (double-click on canvas)
children.comment: Show a contenteditable or input directly on the node card. On blur, call updateNodeField. This is the single most impactful UX improvement.

children.title: Theme persistence (localStorage)
children.comment: Persist the selected theme key to localStorage and rehydrate in the Zustand store initializer. One-line change in mindMapStore.ts using the persist middleware.

children.title: Detail panel themed for Dusk
children.comment: Apply Dusk surface/border tokens to DetailPanel.tsx when theme === dusk.

children.title: Undo / redo (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y)
children.comment: Use zustand/middleware temporal to snapshot rawNodes before destructive actions (deleteNode, addChild). Wire Ctrl+Z to undo and Ctrl+Y to redo in the keydown handler.

children.title: Medium Priority
children.comment: Important for a complete product but not blocking daily use.

children.title: Search / filter nodes
children.comment: A search input in the header that highlights matching nodes and dims non-matching ones. Zoom to first match on Enter.

children.title: Keyboard shortcut — N for new child
children.comment: When a node is selected, pressing N should call addChild(selectedNodeId) and immediately open the detail panel on the new node.

children.title: Drag-and-drop reparenting
children.comment: Allow dragging a node onto another node to reparent it. Requires onNodeDragStop + proximity detection + updateNodeField({ parent_id }).

children.title: Node enter/exit animations (Framer Motion)
children.comment: Wrap node list in AnimatePresence. Each node fades + scales in on mount, fades out on unmount. Requires converting rfNodes updates to trigger AnimatePresence lifecycle.

children.title: Future & Exploratory
children.comment: Longer-horizon ideas that would significantly expand the product's scope.

children.title: AI — generate child nodes from a prompt
children.comment: A prompt field in the detail panel that calls an LLM API (Claude) to suggest N child nodes for the selected node. Results are previewed and imported on confirm.

children.title: Export — PNG / PDF screenshot of canvas
children.comment: Use React Flow's getViewport + html-to-image or a headless browser to capture the canvas. Useful for sharing maps in documents or presentations.

children.title: Multi-user sync (WebSocket)
children.comment: Broadcast node changes over a WebSocket so multiple users on LAN see updates in real time. Requires a diff/patch protocol to avoid full tree re-renders.

children.title: Collections UI — group projects in sidebar
children.comment: The DB already has a collections table and collection_id on projects. The sidebar just needs grouping UI.

children.title: Particle flow edge animation (SVG animateMotion)
children.comment: Custom edge component that renders dots traveling along the SVG path from parent to child. Visually evokes data flowing through a pipeline. Non-trivial: requires a custom ReactFlow edge type.
